does: This paper addresses vulnerabilities in RLHF pipelines by proposing Uncertainty-Aware Reward Modeling (UARM), which enhances reward models with calibrated uncertainty estimates using quantile-based conformal prediction and reweights policy optimization advantages through heteroscedastic variance decomposition. The core contribution is a two-pronged approach: first, reward models output uncertainty estimates alongside predictions to identify unreliable signals; second, these uncertainty estimates are integrated into group-based policy optimization (specifically GRPO) to prevent amplification of unreliable reward signals during advantage computation. The method is evaluated on HelpSteer, UltraFeedback, and PKU-SafeRLHF datasets, demonstrating improvements in reward model calibration and reduced reward hacking artifacts.
connects: This work builds on the established RLHF pipeline for aligning language models, which relies on training preference-based reward models and optimizing policies to maximize predicted rewards. It extends recent developments in group-based policy optimization methods like GRPO that process multiple candidate responses in parallel. The paper responds to known limitations in this framework: the deterministic nature of conventional reward models prevents uncertainty quantification, and uniform treatment of rewards in advantage computation can amplify errors. The approach draws on conformal prediction methods from the uncertainty quantification literature and heteroscedastic variance decomposition from Bayesian machine learning to inject principled uncertainty into the reward modeling pipeline, addressing failure modes identified in prior work on reward hacking and policy drift.
enables: This work enables more robust RLHF systems that can gracefully degrade when faced with distributional shifts or genuinely ambiguous preference signals. By equipping reward models with uncertainty awareness, future systems can implement abstention mechanisms, active learning strategies that request clarification on low-confidence examples, and ensemble-based robustness improvements. The calibrated uncertainty framework opens avenues for developing hybrid policy optimization methods that dynamically adjust exploration based on reward model confidence, potentially enabling safer scaling to more diverse preference distributions. Furthermore, the variance decomposition approach could be extended to other reward modeling architectures and policy optimization algorithms beyond GRPO, establishing uncertainty awareness as a standard component in aligning frontier language models.
